message:
feat(core): Add codon frequency computation for 64-element bias vectors

Extend the bias decomposition module with a complementary codon frequency
function that produces 64-element vectors (all possible codons) in addition
to the existing 16-element dinucleotide vectors.

New function - computeCodonFrequencies(seq):
- Iterates through sequence in 3-base (codon) steps
- Validates each triplet contains only standard nucleotides (ACGT)
- Counts occurrences of all 64 possible codons
- Returns normalized frequency vector in lexicographic codon order

Implementation Details:
- Uses regex validation: /^[ACGT]{3}$/ for codon filtering
- Generates canonical codon order programmatically:
  AAA, AAC, AAG, AAT, ACA, ACC, ACG, ACT, ..., TTT
- Normalizes by total valid codon count
- Returns zeros for missing codons (no smoothing)

Use Cases:
- Codon usage bias comparison between phages
- Detection of codon optimization patterns
- Host adaptation analysis (matching host codon preferences)
- Alternative to dinucleotide analysis for coding regions

The 64-element codon vectors can be passed to decomposeBias() just like
the 16-element dinucleotide vectors, enabling PCA visualization of
codon usage patterns across phage collections.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
